channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: No, There Was Not a Snake Invasion at the Swiss Team’s World Cup Training Facility
item.description: A map of the San Diego facility that the team is using as its base led people to believe that snakes had infiltrated the premises.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin
item.media:credit: Denis Poroy/Associated Press
item.media:description: Switzerland’s training base for this three-country World Cup will be in San Diego.

item.title: Burning Cross Is Found in Downtown Chicago Park
item.description: No one was injured by the burning cross, which was found in Grant Park, the authorities said. The police and the F.B.I. are investigating.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Mark Walker
item.media:credit: Google Maps
item.media:description: The intersection of Balbo and Columbus Drives in Chicago, near where the burning cross was found.

item.title: Top Pentagon Official Worked Closely With C.I.A. Officer Later Found With Gold Bars
item.description: David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, worked on a highly classified China spying program with Stephen A. Feinberg, the Pentagon’s second-ranking official.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Stephen A. Feinberg in February 2025, during his confirmation hearing to be deputy defense secretary.

item.title: Suspect in the Killing of a Minnesota Lawmaker Strikes a Plea Deal
item.description: The arrangement means that Vance Boelter will not face the death penalty after being charged with killing Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Tim Gruber for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Melissa and Mark Hortman at the Minnesota Capitol in St. Paul last year.

item.title: Maine Senate Race Heats Up as Trump Attacks Platner
item.description: With Graham Platner now the Democratic nominee against Senator Susan Collins, signs of how hostile the race will be have quickly emerged.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times, Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner and Senator Susan Collins are beginning what is expected to be a hugely expensive and contentious general election in Maine.

item.title: Democrats Try to Move Past ‘Cultural Pandering’ to Latinos for Midterm Elections
item.description: The party is trying desperately to win back some voters in the key swing group.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times

item.title: University of Florida Chooses Stuart Bell for President Over Some Conservative Criticism
item.description: The nominee, Stuart Bell, led the University of Alabama for a decade, where he embraced diversity initiatives but also Donald J. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Saul and Alan Blinder
item.media:credit: Vasha Hunt/Associated Press
item.media:description: Dr. Bell previously served for a decade as the president of the University of Alabama.

item.title: He has one goooooal.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Mace Defeat Heralds an Exodus of Rabble-Rousers From Congress
item.description: The Republican congresswoman from South Carolina, who made enemies on both sides of the political aisle, is just one of several flashy lawmakers to be leaving Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Nancy Mace, Republican of South Carolina, at the Capitol earlier this year.

item.title: Texas Governor Seeks New Limits on Data Centers
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott is urging tighter controls and a repeal of financial incentives for data centers, which have drawn complaints in rural Republican areas of Texas.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Grapevine, Texas, in March.

item.title: F.B.I. Searches California Plant Where Chemical Tank Nearly Exploded
item.description: Federal agents were looking for records that might shed light on why chemicals overheated, threatening a catastrophe in Orange County.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango and Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:credit: Apu Gomes/Getty Images
item.media:description: An aerial view of water being sprayed onto an overheated 34,000-gallon tank at GKN Aerospace.

item.title: Trump Officials Say ICE Won’t Raid World Cup Games, but Fans Are Worried
item.description: Federal agents are there for security, not immigration enforcement, officials say. But trust is fragile after the administration’s recent crackdowns.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango and Jesus Jiménez
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: Federal agents outside of Dodger Stadium in Los Angeles last June during the Trump administration’s immigration enforcement surge, which has stirred anxiety.

item.title: Southern Baptists Move to Strengthen Ban on Women Pastors
item.description: The amendment passed easily at the denomination’s annual gathering. It needs approval again next year to enter the constitution.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Graham
item.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: R. Albert Mohler Jr. spoke about his proposed amendment at the convention in Orlando, Fla., on Wednesday.

item.title: ‘X-Men’ Actor Tyler Mane Says He Has Breast Cancer
item.description: Tyler Mane said he was initially embarrassed to divulge the diagnosis but wanted to encourage other men to look out for the disease.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai and Dani Blum
item.media:credit: Charly Triballeau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Tyler Mane at the 2024 premiere for “Deadpool & Wolverine.”

item.title: FISA Surveillance Law May Expire After Trump Picks Bill Pulte for Intel Post
item.description: Republicans are struggling to extend a powerful surveillance authority set to lapse this weekend after President Trump alienated lawmakers with his choice of acting spy chief.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Robert Jimison
item.category: Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump last week named Bill Pulte, a confidant without any national security experience, as acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Trump-Backed Air Force Veteran Wins Republican House Primary in Nevada
item.description: David Flippo won his party’s nomination for a safely Republican open seat, defeating a candidate backed by the G.O.P. governor.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson and Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: William Hale Irwin/Associated Press
item.media:description: David Flippo, a Republican candidate for Congress in Nevada’s 2nd district, speaking during a primary election night watch party on Tuesday in Reno, Nev.

item.title: Pro-Palestinian Activists at U. of Michigan Indicted on Federal Conspiracy Charges
item.description: The new charges appeared to signify an escalation in the federal government’s approach to campus activism.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Sylvia Jarrus for The New York Times
item.media:description: The University of Michigan campus in Ann Arbor.

item.title: Could Control of Congress Run Through Northern Maine?
item.description: The state’s vast rural areas, which make up the swing Second Congressional District, will be important in November.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voting on Tuesday in Augusta, Maine.

item.title: Will Trump’s Repairs to Washington D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Work?
item.description: President Trump has said that construction is complete. Now comes the real test. Will the longstanding problems finally be solved?
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: Hegseth Visits Guantánamo Bay Amid U.S. Tensions With Cuba
item.description: The trip comes as the Trump administration pushes for political and economic changes on the island.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Pentagon said the purpose of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s visit to the base at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, was “to engage with troops.”

item.title: NAEP Test Shows Younger Students Recovering From Pandemic
item.description: A national test found surprising progress among 9-year-olds, who are fully caught up in reading. But 13-year-olds have shown no improvement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Mervosh
item.media:credit: Bob Miller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Since 2022, 9-year-olds in the United States have made hopeful gains in reading and math. Student groups that were the most behind saw some of the biggest gains.

item.title: Federal Judge Blocks Alabama From Using Nitrogen Gas in Jeffrey Lee Execution
item.description: The judge ordered the state to find an alternate method if it is going to move ahead with the execution of Jeffery Lee, who was convicted of murder, and an appeals court upheld the decision.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Jake Crandall/Advertiser, via USA TODAY NETWORK, via Imagn Images
item.media:description: A mask similar to those used in nitrogen hypoxia executions was held up during a protest against the death penalty outside the Alabama State Capitol in Montgomery, Ala., last year.

item.title: ActBlue C.E.O. Invokes Fifth Amendment Repeatedly in Testimony to Congress
item.description: The head of the Democratic fund-raising behemoth declined to answer questions from House Republicans about reports that she may have misled Congress over how her group vetted foreign donations.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Regina Wallace-Jones, the chief executive of ActBlue, was questioned on Wednesday by House Republicans including Representative Jim Jordan of Ohio, pictured in the background.

item.title: Accused Palisades Arsonist Resented the Wealthy, Prosecutor Says
item.description: A defense lawyer said in opening statements that no evidence linked the suspect directly to the blaze that, a week later, would burn out of control.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto and Vik Jolly
item.media:credit: Jae C. Hong/Associated Press
item.media:description: Homes were under construction in the Pacific Palisades neighborhood of Los Angeles, where fire burned through in early 2025.

item.title: The Knicks Are Hogging the Spotlight. Spurs Fans Aren’t Impressed.
item.description: As New York basks in the attention of a long-awaited N.B.A. finals run, many San Antonians say the Spurs, and the city they represent, are being underestimated.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval
item.media:credit: Christopher Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Spurs fans at an outdoor watch party at The Pearl for Game 3 of the NBA Finals in San Antonio.

item.title: Graham Platner’s Victory in Maine Senate Primary Gives Democrats Hope and Fears
item.description: Graham Platner’s primary victory sets up a high-stakes contest between an insurgent progressive with political baggage and a battle-tested but vulnerable Republican senator.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Graham Platner will face Senator Susan Collins, a moderate Republican, in a general election in Maine that is seen as central to the fight for Senate control.

item.title: Life Inside an ICE Detention Facility
item.description: For days, protesters have been clashing with law enforcement over conditions at an ICE detention center in New Jersey. The New York Times reporter Hamed Aleaziz explains how these complaints persist across the country and takes a closer look at the problems documented at one center in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Jon Hazell, Gilad Thaler, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Bees Are Swarming Earlier: What to Do if You’re Attacked
item.description: Experts discuss the threat and how to escape aggressive bees. Hint: Don’t jump in water.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser
item.media:credit: Matt York/Associated Press
item.media:description: A swarm of bees landed on the protective netting behind home plate during a baseball game in Phoenix in April 2024.

item.title: How the Epstein Files Paralyzed the Trump White House
item.description: President Trump’s advisers gathered in secret in the Situation Room without him as they struggled to handle the Epstein files scandal, our reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan learned while researching their book, “Regime Change.” Here’s the inside story. Correction: An earlier version of this video mislabeled the date of Pam Bondi’s interview clip.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan, John Pappas, Jon Miller, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, James Surdam, Paul Abowd, Gilad Thaler, Whitney Shefte and Luke Piotrowski

item.title: A Catholic Mayor Commissioned Statues of Saints. Residents Revolted.
item.description: The mayor of Quincy, Mass., is being sued over 10-foot statues of two Catholic saints that he wants to install in front of a new public safety complex.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
